definition:
κολλύριον, see: κολλούριον.
 κολλούριον
 (TTr., κολλύ-, the more usual form), -ου, τό (dimin. of κολλύρα = κόλλιξ, a coarse bread roll), [in LXX: 3Ki.12:24 hil B* ;] 
  1. a small bread roll (LXX, ll. with). 
   2. (Usually in pl., LS, see word) an eye salve shaped like a roll: Rev.3:18.†
 (AS)
